title: Clavija pungens
intro: Clavija pungens, also known as the "Pungent Clavija", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Desmodialeae, within the Annonaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Clavija pungens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Clavija pungens include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory infections and fever.
